top_signals.title: US CPI Falls for the First Time Since 2020
top_signals.summary: The US consumer price index fell 0.4% in June, dragged down by the biggest decline in gasoline prices since 2022, taking some pressure off the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates. Tiffany Wilding, economist at Pimco, examines the data on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
